$comment: Driver character system — synthesized model (docs/dev/character-system.md). User-editable, validated on load. Every perk shapes a DETERMINISTIC sim INPUT (player-seat PackDriverRatings, the player's own car weight/power/drag scalars, offer-scoring weights, salary/BU, aging/injury hazard, XP gain) or an OUTPUT weight (results -> OPI/reputation/XP). No perk decides a race; perks that add randomness name a registered PCG32 stream keyed per (subsystem, year, round, entity) exactly like CareerStreams. HYBRID balance: a creation CP budget prices each perk (drawback-heavy perks REFUND CP, negative cost), and independently every perk pairs a quantified benefit with a quantified drawback on a different axis so free choice is genuinely viable. Every perk's benefit AND drawback are expressed as machine-readable effects[] so the audit can prove Sum(cpEquivalent) ~= cost and that no perk is strictly-dominant or strictly-trap. Lever vocabulary + effect schema: see docs/dev/character-system.md sec 7.

characterPoints.$comment: PERKS: an archetype IS your identity (3 signature perks); creation should add only a FEW more. TWO limits enforce that: (1) maxPerks = at most 5 perks total, so archetype + about two picks; (2) a 6 CP budget - positive-cost perks spend it, negative-cost (drawback-heavy) perks refund up to +3 over budget so a fragile/greedy build can still afford one premium upside. Net perk spend must land in [0, budget+maxRefundHeadroom]. STATS: the seven stats may sum to at most statSumCap (neutral is 7*0.5=3.5); redistribution below the cap is free AT CREATION, so a driver can be great somewhere by being weak elsewhere, but NO driver is great at everything. IN-CAREER you only RAISE stats with earned points - never trade one rating for another. The 13 archetype presets all sum <= the cap; maxing all seven (5.95) is impossible. Between-season level grants add CP to spend on new perks or +0.05 stat steps.

stats.$comment: Seven stats, each 0..1, clamped 0.15..0.85 at creation. Five talent stats write PackDriverRatings on the player seat via writtenRating = clamp(writeBase + writeSpan*stat + perkDeltas, 0, 1). They set the sim's EXPECTATION of the player (expectedFinish), the difficulty recommendation, and the fiction - AMS2 ignores AI skill fields for the human-driven seat, so higher talent => a HARDER OPI/rep/XP bar (the built-in self-balancer), never a secret in-race buff. Two meta-stats have no rating analog and modulate the offer/economy and injury/aging levers.

talentStats.$comment: Feeds SeatStrengthModel.Strength (0.3 weight) -> expectedFinish -> OPI/rep bar; PaceAnchorMath yardstick + MedianAiRaceSkill; DifficultyModel target.
Grid-pace identity; splits from pace when a perk buys it.
Fewer driver-error DNFs in fiction; pairs with OPI blame-softening perks.
Overtaking/defending narrative + beat-teammate / overperformed headline causes.
Round-conditional: bites on wet / long-distance rounds.

metaStats.$comment: NOT a custom-AI field. Pre-multiplier 1.0 + 0.5*(marketability-0.5) on ReputationMath deltas + a scalar on the rep term into OfferScore and SalaryOffer. A fame lever, not a pace lever.

NOT a custom-AI field. ageShift = round(6*(durability-0.5)) years into RetirementHazard.Probability and the ageRisk offer term; scales the opt-in season-end injury roll (stream 'injury'). A longevity lever, not a pace lever.

levels.$comment: XP is a pure function of journaled results (no dice, no stream) - replay reproduces every level byte-for-byte. XP -> levels -> Character Points spent between seasons on a new perk or a +0.05 stat step. Leveling is the counterweight to the shipped AgingCurve: early climb, peak plateau, veteran decline you spend CP holding station against.

xpCurve.formula: xpForLevel(n) = round(baseXpToLevel2 * growth^(n-2)); cumulative thresholds precomputed and validated strictly increasing on load.

xpSources.$comment: Keyed to journal causes RoundUpdate.RaceCause and SeasonEndPipeline already emit. New pure XpMath emits a player.xp row per round + one in season-end step 2.

perRound.$comment: xpRound = clamp((expectedFinish - effectiveFinish) * finishVsExpectedPerPlace, floor, cap) + resultBonus(cause). Overperforming in a weak car is the richest XP - mirrors the reputation underdog logic so a backmarker career levels.

levelGrants.$comment: LEVELING is the main way stats grow. Each level -> 3 CP (raisable by consummate_pro). 1 CP = one +0.05 stat step (RAISE only, up to statCapPerRating 0.99 - ABOVE the creation band, so a driver grows past where they started) OR banked toward a perk. Every 5th level grants a respec token. Spending is journaled (player.level / player.statSpend); the sim never auto-spends.

softCapByEra.$comment: Ties the reachable level ceiling to the era so a 1967 career and a 2022 career reach comparable ceilings; author-editable, never compiled. Absent era => maxLevel applies.

respec.respecTokenScope: one perk swap ONLY (for a perk of EQUAL OR LOWER cost, difference refunded as CP). In-career you never trade stat points between ratings - grown stats are locked (statPointsLockedOnSpend) and only ever RAISED with earned points.
respec.$comment: Long-arc integrity: choices matter (perks semi-permanent) without punishing lock-in. Equal-or-lower-cost swap rule blocks token-laundering into a strictly-stronger build, keeping the creation-time balance audit valid for the whole career. Journaled 'player.respec'.

streams.$comment: New named PCG32 streams (same construction as CareerStreams: SplitMix64(Fnv1a64(subsystem|year|round|entity) XOR masterSeed)). Register as consts in CareerStreams.cs when building. All are gated/opt-in so a default career consumes ZERO new draws and stays replay-compatible with pre-character saves.

accident.$comment: Character death & injury §3.4: the accident d500 outcome bands (1 unit = 0.2%) + safety-offset scales. upTo = inclusive upper bound on the SAFETY-ADJUSTED effective roll (higher = more dangerous); the first band the effective roll does not exceed wins, and the last band must cover 500. safetyDurabilityScale shifts the roll toward safety by (durability + injury-durability perks); safetyBaseAddScale shifts it toward danger by a reckless injury baseAdd (e.g. hot_head). Mirrors AccidentModel.DefaultRules — omit this whole block to fall back to that code default.

creation.$comment: Fits the wizard as one WizardStep.Character AFTER SeatPick, BEFORE RulesPreset. Three tiers: (1) archetype preset = a pre-spent, in-budget point template (one click = a complete valid character); (2) free customize - drag the 7 stat sliders (0.15..0.85) and swap/add perks with a live remaining-CP meter; (3) advanced - raw stat->rating numbers + exact lever deltas. A 'Random balanced build' button draws the 'character-gen' stream (seeded off masterSeed, journaled once). Confirm writes one 'player.character' journal row {stats, perkIds, cpSpent}.

archetypes.name: The Prodigy
archetypes.description: A generational talent thrust into a top seat too young. Blistering early pace and a fast-levelling arc, but the fame is fragile and the crashes come.

archetypes.name: The Late Bloomer
archetypes.description: Overlooked for years, you keep improving when your peers fade. A long, ascending veteran career built on craft and durability rather than raw youth.

archetypes.name: The All-Rounder
archetypes.description: No glaring weakness and no towering strength. The safe, adaptable professional who is competitive in any car and any era.

archetypes.name: The Pay Driver
archetypes.description: You bought the seat, not earned it. Sponsor money and glamour open doors that talent alone would not - and the paddock never lets you forget it.

archetypes.name: The Rain Master
archetypes.description: When the heavens open you are untouchable; when the track dries you are ordinary. A specialist who lives and dies by the forecast.

archetypes.name: The Journeyman
archetypes.description: A grizzled pair of hands who has driven everything and knows the market. Trusted, cheap, durable - a survivor, not a star.

archetypes.name: The Hard Charger
archetypes.description: Wheel-to-wheel and fearless, the crowd's favourite and the stewards' problem. Raw speed and raw risk in equal measure.

archetypes.name: The Privateer
archetypes.description: You bought the entry, built the car in your own lock-up and pay the bills yourself. Sponsor money keeps the lights on and a spanner in your own hand keeps you honest - a genuine racer-owner scraping a living at the back of the grid.

archetypes.name: The Comeback Veteran
archetypes.description: Written off years ago, you came back leaner and wiser than the paddock remembered. Fit as a fiddle and loyal to a fault, you keep drawing offers long after your peers hung up the helmet - even if the ultimate ceiling is behind you.

archetypes.name: The Storm Chaser
archetypes.description: A wet-weather artist who thrives when the skies turn and the race runs long. You reinvent yourself for every changing condition and nurse the car to the flag - but on a bone-dry sprint you are just another midfield runner.

archetypes.name: The Pole Sitter
archetypes.description: A Saturday hero. You brake later than anyone and hang it on pole with metronomic regularity - the drama comes on qualifying day, and Sunday is a matter of holding station.

archetypes.name: The Points Machine
archetypes.description: Unspectacular and unbreakable. You are never the story, but you are always there at the flag banking the points while the stars break down - a durable, metronomic professional the standings quietly reward.

archetypes.name: The Showman
archetypes.description: Chaos is your stage and the cameras adore you. When others crash you are already up the road, and a breakout weekend sends your stock soaring - but a quiet one just as quickly forgotten. Feast or famine, always in the headlines.

perks.name: Sunday Driver
perks.description: You come alive on race day. What you give up over one flying lap you claw back when it counts, wheel to wheel through the pack.

effects.note: higher expectedFinish contribution (0.3 weight)
rating mass redistributed, not gifted - starts further back

perks.name: Saturday Specialist
perks.description: A flying-lap artist. Pole position is your art form; converting it on Sunday is somebody else's.

effects.note: grid-pace identity + briefing aid
lower race-day expectation
a good grid slot raises expectedFinish, shrinking overperformance XP

perks.name: Glass Cannon
perks.description: A knife-edge machine tuned for pure speed. Devastating on its day, and one mistake from the barrier.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): the benefit is +0.015 power = +0.09 Strength REAL on-track pace EVERY round unconditionally, but the original drawbacks lived ENTIRELY on the opt-in 'injury' stream, so with the injury system disabled the perk became a pure-upside refund (strictly dominant). Fix: (1) a NON-INJURY, always-live drawback (fragile setup = avoidanceOfMistakes -0.10 + errorBlameScale +0.10) that alone justifies most of the refund whether or not injury is on; (2) the injury effects auto-enable the injury system for this character (spec sec 6.2), so the injury half is never a disabled freebie; (3) benefit trimmed 0.015 -> 0.012 so even with injury fully off the net is a genuine risk/reward, not a free +0.09 Strength.

effects.note: +~0.12 CarScore (Strength +0.072), ~a third of a tier of real on-track pace, unconditional
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): a knife-edge car bites back - materially more driver-error DNF exposure regardless of the injury setting
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): errorBlameScale 1.10 - a crash off the edge costs 10% more OPI than the grid size, so mistakes are punished harder even with injury off
INJURY (auto-enables the injury system for this character): treated -0.20 durability for the season-end injury roll
INJURY: each driver-error DNF adds +0.15 to that round's injury contribution

perks.name: Featherweight Setup
perks.description: You run the car light and nimble. It rewards you through the corners and punishes you down the straights - a gamble on the calendar's shape.

effects.note: less weight = quicker (CarRating subtracts weight); +~0.10 CarScore
more drag = slower; -~0.10 CarScore. Net ~0 across a mixed calendar, positive only on downforce circuits - situational

perks.name: Engineer's Favourite
perks.description: A quiet technician who wrings every tenth from the setup sheet. A small, honest edge on every track - and no glamour to show for it.

effects.note: small permanent real pace edge on every track; +~0.10 CarScore
+~0.08 CarScore
a technician, not a launcher
quiet and unglamorous - rep grows slower; small enough that the pace anchor barely re-rates you

perks.name: Test Driver
perks.description: Thousands of development laps taught you to dial a car in fast. New machinery clicks for you sooner - but teams see a tester, not a racer.

effects.note: alpha 0.3 -> 0.45: the recommended slider + OPI expectation converge quicker in a new car/era
experience term x0.85 - read as a tester
slightly weaker overtaking narrative

perks.name: Late Braker
perks.description: You brake deeper into the corner than anyone dares. It buys you positions on entry - and flat-spots, lock-ups and the occasional overshoot.

effects.note: stronger overtaking narrative + overperformed headline chance
small real entry-pace edge; +~0.06 CarScore
flat-spots and locked tyres cost you on long runs

perks.name: Hard Charger
perks.description: A wheel-to-wheel monster. Every overtake reads as a headline - and every clumsy one reads as a stewards' inquiry.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): two defects. (1) top-level stream 'none' contradicted an effect's 'injury' stream - corrected to 'injury' so the loader registers it. (2) at cost 2 the perk was overpriced: aggression/defending are RATING-ONLY (inert on track - they drive fiction/rep/beat-teammate and self-tax via expectation, not real lap time), worth ~2.4 CP of benefit, but a wheel-to-wheel style needs real always-live crash drawbacks (~-1.4) to not be injury-off-dominant. 2.4 - 1.4 - 0.4(injury) = +0.6 ~= cost 1, not 2. Recosted to 1. The always-live avoidance+errorBlame half carries the price whether or not injury is on (injury-off net +1.0 ~= cost 1, never dominant). Archetype 'hard_charger' net falls 1->0, still in budget.

effects.note: overtaking machine; tags overperformed hero copy (rating-only - drives fiction/rep/beat-teammate, self-taxes via expectation)
hard to pass, too
ALWAYS-LIVE: a hard charger crashes more - materially more driver-error DNF exposure whether or not injury is enabled
ALWAYS-LIVE: errorBlameScale 1.08 - the stewards blame the aggressor, so a crash costs more OPI
INJURY (auto-enables the injury system): a dangerous style - +0.06 base injury hazard; a small extra, NOT load-bearing (perk is cost-justified by the always-live drawbacks alone)

perks.name: Hothead
perks.description: All fire and no filter. You force moves that shouldn't work - and when they don't, you pay for them twice.

effects.note: stronger overtaking narrative + small expectedFinish bump
errorBlameScale 1.15 - a crash is charged 15% beyond gridSize (capped): a mistake costs more OPI

perks.name: Ice in the Veins
perks.description: Ruthlessly calm. You almost never bin it, and the rare day you do the paddock forgives you - but you never force the improbable move.

effects.note: far fewer driver-error DNFs in fiction
errorBlameScale 0.85 - a rare mistake blames you less
you don't force it - slower rep climb, fewer overperformed bonuses

perks.name: Safe Hands
perks.description: You drive within yourself and bring it home. Teams love the reliability; the stopwatch notices the tenths you leave on the table.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): floorBlend benefit is non-injury and always-live, but the injury durabilityDelta was pulling 0.6 CP of the pricing, so with injury off the perk paid 1 CP for ~0.4 CP of net value (mild trap). Fix: the always-live half (floorBlend + a new avoidanceOfMistakes flavor) now covers the cost; durability is trimmed and labelled UPSIDE; the pace drawback is the honest 'tenths left on the table'.

effects.note: ALWAYS-LIVE: driver-error DNFs charged as (gridSize+expectedFinish)/2 instead of full gridSize
ALWAYS-LIVE: fewer driver-error DNFs in the fiction
INJURY UPSIDE (auto-enables the injury system): safer style - lower injury roll; a bonus, not load-bearing
ALWAYS-LIVE: pace left on the table; -~0.08 CarScore (Strength -0.048)
ALWAYS-LIVE: you never force the improbable move - fewer overperformed/beat-teammate moments
ALWAYS-LIVE: -10% win XP - the highs are rarer

perks.name: Opportunist
perks.description: Chaos is a ladder. When others crash and the safety car flies, you are already three places up the road - as long as you gambled right.

effects.note: when rivals melt down (attrition rounds), your round rep gain +20% via the form-swing classification
first-lap positioning
you gamble on chaos - steadier, safer results are worth less to you and variance is higher

perks.name: Ironman
perks.description: Built to last. Injuries barely slow you and the years are kind - but all that bulk is weight the car has to carry.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): the load-bearing benefit was an injury-stream durabilityDelta +0.25 (cpEq 1.8). With injury disabled that benefit evaporated, leaving ageRisk-0.15 (1.2) against a REAL weight+0.010 = -0.06 Strength every round (-1.0) - you paid 2 CP for a net PACE LOSS => a trap when injury is off. Fix: the always-live ageRisk benefit now carries most of the value; the weight drawback is softened (0.010 -> 0.006) so the non-injury floor (~+0.9 CP of value for cost 2 is still a mild tax but no longer a strict trap - the whole point is you accept a pace tax for longevity); durability is now clearly labelled UPSIDE when injury is enabled. Also adds an always-live agingCurve declineAccel benefit so the longevity fantasy is real even with injury off.

effects.note: ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): ageRisk term x0.80 - teams fear the clock less, offers keep coming late
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): a body built to last - DeclineAccelPerYear x0.60, a gentler veteran tail whether or not the injury system is on
INJURY UPSIDE (auto-enables the injury system): far fewer lost rounds; injuries never remove more than 1 round - a bonus on top of an already cost-justified perk, never load-bearing
ALWAYS-LIVE: heavier car - a permanent pace tax; -~0.06 CarScore (Strength -0.036)
ALWAYS-LIVE: bulk over finesse - fades a touch on the very longest runs
ALWAYS-LIVE: a durable grinder is unglamorous - rep banks a touch slower

perks.name: Iron Constitution
perks.description: The ageless veteran. You race well past the era's peak age and keep drawing offers when your peers are long retired - at the cost of a lower ultimate ceiling.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): 1.8 of the 3.7 CP of benefit sat on injury-stream durabilityDelta +0.30. With injury disabled you paid 3 CP for peakShift+2 (1.2) + ageRisk (0.7) = 1.9 CP of value minus a real softCap drawback (-0.7) => net ~1.2 for cost 3, a trap. Fix: the ALWAYS-LIVE longevity benefits (peakShift, ageRisk, and a new declineAccel halving) now carry the full cost on their own; the injury durability is trimmed and re-labelled UPSIDE. The softCap drawback is the honest price of a long plateau over a high peak (pairs with the longevity axis).

effects.note: ALWAYS-LIVE: player-local peak +3 years - decline starts later
ALWAYS-LIVE: DeclineAccelPerYear halved - the classic ageless-veteran tail, real whether or not injury is enabled
ALWAYS-LIVE: ageRisk term x0.80 - offers keep coming long past the peak
INJURY UPSIDE (auto-enables the injury system): durable grinder - low injury roll; a bonus, never load-bearing
ALWAYS-LIVE: peak ceiling capped - no rating above 0.89 - a longer plateau, not a higher peak
ALWAYS-LIVE: a solid, unspectacular veteran's setup - a small permanent pace tax; -~0.08 CarScore (Strength -0.048)

perks.name: Injury Prone
perks.description: Blindingly quick and made of glass. You sell your body's fragility to buy raw talent - and hope the season doesn't send the bill.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): the entire refund was carried by an injury-stream durabilityDelta -0.30. With the injury system disabled that drawback evaporated, leaving raceSkill+0.06/qual+0.04 for a 2-CP refund minus only ageRisk - grossly dominant. Fix: keep a real ALWAYS-LIVE drawback (offerWeight ageRisk up materially: fragile reputation scares teams whether or not a bone actually breaks) that carries most of the refund, and cap the injury half so it is upside-risk, not the whole cost. raceSkill benefit is expectation-only (inert on track), so it self-taxes via a higher OPI bar too.

effects.note: raw talent bought at the cost of fragility - expectation-only (inert on track), so it also raises the OPI/rep bar
grid-pace identity
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): a body-of-glass reputation makes teams treat you as an age-risk from day one - ageRisk term counts ~35% heavier, drying up offers regardless of the injury setting
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): a driver the paddock expects to break down never fully builds a legacy - season rep bonus x0.90

INJURY (auto-enables the injury system for this character): retirement/injury hazard sharply up; a hit forces an OPI-neutral missed round via the 'injury' stream - never sets finishing order, fully journaled/replayable

perks.name: Hot Head (Fitness)
perks.description: You drive on adrenaline and burn hot. Explosive launches and a fearless first lap - and a body that pays for the risk.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): benefit power+0.012 = +0.072 Strength REAL on-track pace unconditionally, but ~2.7 CP of the ~3.7 CP drawback sat on the opt-in injury stream (baseAdd + perErrorAdd). With injury off the perk kept only a small rep-on-error drawback and refunded 2 CP for real pace - dominant. Fix: an always-live avoidanceOfMistakes drawback (a reckless style crashes more, injury or not) plus the rep-on-error drawback carry the refund without injury; injury effects trimmed to upside-risk and auto-enable the system.

effects.note: +~0.12 CarScore (Strength +0.072) of raw aggression pace, unconditional real on-track
rocket launches
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): burning hot means more driver-error DNFs whether or not you get hurt
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): a crash costs an extra rep hit on top of OPI blame
ALWAYS-LIVE (non-injury): errorBlameScale 1.08 - the paddock blames the hot-head first
INJURY (auto-enables the injury system): +0.05 base injury hazard from a reckless style
INJURY: each error adds +0.10 to the injury roll

perks.name: Stamina Freak
perks.description: Fitter than anyone on the grid. In the closing laps of a long race you are still hitting your marks while the field wilts - but the short sprints never test you.

effects.note: fiction + condition tag
you don't fade - +~0.10 CarScore on long (>=60% distance) rounds
no fitness edge in a sprint, and you over-drive it - -~0.06 CarScore on short rounds

perks.name: Consistency King
perks.description: A metronome. Good form sticks to you and a single bad Sunday barely dents your standing - but a breakout day doesn't rocket you up the market either.

effects.note: OPI retention 0.8 -> 0.85: form persists, one bad round hurts less
steadier finishes near expectation
the same higher retention means a breakout moves OPI less (gain 0.2 -> 0.15) - stickier both ways
effects.$comment: modeled as the paired gain-side reduction of the same retention lever

perks.name: Streaky
perks.description: Feast or famine. A breakout weekend sends you soaring up the market; a bad one can make your offers evaporate. High risk, high reward, no middle gear.

effects.note: retention 0.7 (gain 0.3): overperformances spike OPI fast, accelerating offer-score (opi weight up to 3.0 at works)
the same 0.7 retention means one bad round tanks OPI hard - offers can evaporate in a season; higher season-end variance. Refunds CP for the risk.

perks.name: Metronome
perks.description: Utterly unshakeable. Nothing you do surprises anyone - for better and for worse. The steadiest climb on the grid, and the least dramatic.

effects.note: retention 0.8 -> 0.86: near-immune to a single bad round
a breakout barely moves OPI - lowest variance both ways; a pure temperament trait

perks.name: Prodigy
perks.description: You arrived fully formed and dazzled from day one - then reality caught up. A blazing start, a visible sophomore slump, and a lifetime's talent that nets out about even.

effects.note: while seasonsCompleted < 3 / pre-peak: +0.05 raceSkill for expectedFinish - a fast start
after the window the bonus reverses to a two-season slump (authored decay); net lifetime rating mass ~0, front-loaded

perks.name: Student of the Craft
perks.description: You learn most from the grim days. A DNF or a midfield slog teaches you plenty; a win teaches you nothing you didn't already know.

effects.note: +50% XP from midfield finishes (the bulk of most careers)
+50% XP from mechanical DNFs - you learn from every lap
success breeds complacency
neutral over a mixed career, strong for a backmarker grind

perks.name: Sponsor Magnet
perks.description: You bring a chequebook and a logo the money-hungry teams can't refuse. Doors open up and down the paddock - except at the top, where they see a pay driver.

effects.note: +2.0 BU/season pay budget that privateer/minnow archetypes weight (PayDriverWeight) - materially more offers from money-hungry teams
works teams score your rep at 0.85x - the big teams doubt a chequebook driver
seen as expensive baggage at merit teams

perks.name: Cheap Contract
perks.description: You'll drive for peanuts. Every skint team on the grid can afford you, so the offers pour in - but your bank balance never grows.

effects.note: salaryAsk x0.6 - a much smaller w4 subtraction, so more teams clear their threshold, especially minnows (salary weight 3.5)
accepted salary x0.6 - less BU banked (matters for the Phase-2 ledger and BU-gated perks)
cheap = low prestige

perks.name: Prima Donna
perks.description: A star who knows their worth and charges for it. The pay is superb and the fame follows - but the thrifty end of the grid can't afford your tantrums.

effects.note: salary offers interpolate at 1.25x
fame-driven podium reputation
salaryAsk term x1.4 - wage demands cost you thrifty privateer/minnow offers; with a minnow (salary weight 3.5) it can cost you the seat
complacency - slightly more variance

perks.name: Mercenary
perks.description: No loyalty, only leverage. You chase the best seat every winter and never look back - which makes you rich in offers and poor in patience from any one team.

effects.note: you spike your market value fast off a hot run (gain 0.2 -> 0.25)
you always squeeze the wage
no team trusts a job-hopper's loyalty - experience term x0.8
no team-building legacy - season rep bonus x0.85

perks.name: Company Man
perks.description: You put down roots. Stay with a team and their faith in you compounds; the price is a wage that never keeps up with the mercenaries and a market that assumes you'll never leave.

effects.note: loyalty rewarded - experience term x1.3, more mid-career offers
a legacy builds - season rep bonus x1.15
loyal = underpaid - salary x0.85
the market assumes you'll re-sign cheap and doesn't chase you

perks.name: Rain Man
perks.description: When the rain comes you drive on another plane entirely. When it stays dry you are merely mortal - a specialist at the mercy of the sky.

effects.note: towering wet-round identity
real pace edge ONLY on wet rounds; +~0.20 CarScore
dry-weather complacency; -~0.08 CarScore. Wet rounds are the calendar minority so this bites most weekends
adaptability average drops on dry rounds

perks.name: Sunshine Specialist
perks.description: A fair-weather artist. In the dry your car placement is millimetre-perfect; the moment it rains, all that precision deserts you.
perks.$auditNote: REBALANCED (balance audit 2026-07-05): at dry +0.010 / wet -0.030 the calendar-expected on-track Strength was slightly POSITIVE on a typical dry-majority calendar (0.8*+0.06 + 0.2*-0.18 = +0.012), making a cost-0 perk mildly dominant on dry-heavy packs. Deepened the wet penalty to -0.040 so the expectation is genuinely ~neutral-to-negative (0.8*+0.06 + 0.2*-0.24 = +0.0 exactly at ~20% wet, negative on wetter packs) - a true situational playstyle trait, dominant on no calendar. Magnitude -0.040 stays within the +/-0.015... note: carScalar per-lever cap in the spec is +/-0.015 per EFFECT, but conditional weather swings are the intended exception (rain_man already runs +0.020/-0.008); the wet minority makes the calendar-weighted delta small. If the loader enforces a hard +/-0.015 carScalar cap, split this into two -0.020 wet effects or raise the documented weather-conditional cap to +/-0.040 (see spec sec 4.1d).

effects.note: real pace edge on the dry majority of rounds; +~0.10 CarScore
you can't feel the grip in the wet; -~0.40 CarScore on the rare wet round - deep enough that the calendar-weighted expectation is neutral, never a free cost-0 edge
openly weak in the rain

perks.name: Tyre Whisperer
perks.description: You make a set of tyres last a lifetime. In a long race you come alive as others crawl to the flag on ruined rubber - but there's nothing to save in a sprint.

effects.note: long-run master
tyre saving = end-of-race pace on long (>=60% distance) rounds; +~0.12 CarScore
no thermal window in a sprint, and over-managing costs you; -~0.10 CarScore

perks.name: Fuel Saver
perks.description: A master of the economy run. When the tank matters you eke out an advantage nobody else can find - and when it doesn't, you're just lifting and coasting for nothing.

effects.note: economy master
run lighter on fuel = quicker on long rounds; +~0.08 CarScore
sprint flat-out means your economy habit just costs lap time; -~0.12 CarScore

perks.name: Underdog Hero
perks.description: Heroics in a hopeless car make your name faster than anyone's. But once you finally land a good seat, the same drives that were miracles are merely expected.

effects.note: +0.25 to UnderdogMultiplier in tier<=2 seats - rep banks fast in a minnow (the common early-career seat)
round + season rep x0.85 in tier>=4 seats - top-team results impress less, capping the snowball

perks.name: Journeyman
perks.description: Everyone's known you for years and everyone trusts you. More doors open than your results deserve - until the miles on the clock finally scare the teams off all at once.

effects.note: experience term x1.5 for the player
rep floors relaxed one tier - more teams talk to a veteran
ageRisk term counts double - a sharper late-career cliff

perks.name: Works Prodigy
perks.description: Factory-honed and marked as a genuine front-runner. The raw pace is real and the works teams covet you - but the game will always ask more of you to prove it.

effects.note: genuine pace lift to SeatStrengthModel.Strength and a real front-runner identity
front-runner grid pace

the raised expectedFinish is the built-in self-tax: you must beat a stiffer expected finish for the same OPI/rep/XP, and the difficulty recommendation rises with your talent - modeled here as a -15% rep gain the career must overcome

perks.name: Team Player
perks.description: A model professional inside the garage. The engineers give you their best and your team-mate their data - but you'll defer on strategy when it matters, and rarely take the ruthless win.

effects.note: the crew's best setup - a small real pace edge; +~0.06 CarScore
a trusted operator
you defer, you don't force the issue - fewer overperformed/beat-teammate moments
the selfless number-two banks acclaim slower

perks.name: Media Darling
perks.description: The press adore you and the sponsors follow. Your fame - and pay - climb faster than your results, but stardom prices you out of the cheap seats you might need on the way down.

effects.note: scales the rep term into OfferScore + nudges SalaryOffer up ~10%
the glamour amplifies every result's rep
salaryAsk raised ~15%: with a works team (salary weight 1.0) it nets positive, with a minnow (weight 3.5) it can net NEGATIVE and cost you the seat

perks.name: Quiet Professional
perks.description: You let the stopwatch do the talking. The press ignore you and your fame crawls - but you never say the wrong thing, and cost-conscious teams love a driver with no entourage.

effects.note: no star wages - a smaller w4 subtraction, so thrifty privateer/minnow teams court you
no distractions
rep climbs ~10% slower - the rep-floor gates to top seats stay shut longer despite the pace
low profile = modest pay

perks.name: Superstition
perks.description: A driver of rituals and lucky charms. It makes for wonderful copy - and a form that wobbles from race to race for reasons no engineer can chart.

effects.note: colourful character - rich journal/headline flavour and a small fame bump

a deterministic per-round form wobble via the 'form-swing' stream nudges your effective consistency +/-0.05 for that round only (journaled), so expected-finish stability wavers race to race - fully replayable, never your choice, sometimes helps, sometimes bites; refunds CP for the volatility

perks.name: Late Bloomer
perks.description: You peak when others are already declining. Your best years come late and last long - but you start closer to your ceiling and climb to it slowly.

effects.note: player-local PeakAgeStart/End +3 - decline starts later, a long career holds its expectedFinish
+25% XP once past peak - keep growing when others fade
-30% XP while young - an unremarkable youth (this IS the 'slow early growth'); total XP ~neutral, back-loaded

perks.name: Wonderkid
perks.description: You burst onto the scene fully formed and improved faster than anyone thought possible - and then the well ran dry at your peak while the grinders kept climbing.

effects.note: +40% per-round XP while age < eraPeakAgeStart - front-loads the whole growth arc
-25% per-round XP once at/past peak - the spigot slams shut; net lifetime XP ~neutral, front-loaded

perks.name: Adaptable
perks.description: You reinvent yourself every time the machinery changes. Era transitions that break other drivers barely touch you - but chasing every new trend means you never fully master one.

effects.note: player-local DeclineAccelPerYear halved - a graceful, long veteran tail
keep +0.03 raceSkill at each era transition that re-baselining would otherwise strip - compounds across a multi-era career
-15% all XP - effort spread thin adapting; longevity bought with a lower ceiling

perks.name: One-Trick Pony
perks.description: You have one weapon, honed to perfection, and nothing else. That single specialism is untouchable - and it is the only thing about you that will ever grow.

effects.note: one chosen flavor rating locked high at creation (free, outside creationRange) + level points auto-assign to it
that rating is the ONLY flavor stat level points may raise - every other flavor stat frozen at creation value forever; refunds CP but kills build flexibility
